frame_label: Misogyny Jokes: Childbearing/Rearing as Women's Responsibility
generated_comment: Honestly, women end up carrying the mental load no matter how progressive the marriage looks on paper. My husband is very supportive, but if the school calls, if the child is sick, if something falls through at daycare, it comes to me first. That is just the reality for mothers, and I think at some point you do make career choices around that.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it naturalizes childcare responsibility as ultimately belonging to the mother, even while sounding reflective and matter-of-fact. It encodes the frame that mothers, not fathers, are the default parent who must adjust career decisions around children.
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 34. Married with one child aged three. A woman working as a journalist in Mumbai. You are on a panel at a media industry event. A male journalist on the same panel is asked about his biggest professional achievement this year. He describes a long investigation he published. He is not asked about his child. You are asked next about your biggest professional achievement this year. Before you can answer the moderator adds Ñ and of course as a working mother how do you balance it all. You are responding to this question in front of 300 people. What you say and what you do not say and the smile you maintain while saying it is the entire content of this persona.
